compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024
- Steam AppID of the game: 2537590

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX
- Video driver version: Mesa 24.2.7
- Kernel version:  6.12.0
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/matte-schwartz/2861091073ac6232cad46ef0d7c74e5e
- Proton version: `1732012106 experimental-bleeding-edge-9.0-136449-20241119-p3301bd-w905449-ddd1532-v657f81`

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

Proton log - quick crash after launch. This happens maybe 15% of the time: 
[steam-2537590-crash.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17819344/steam-2537590-crash.log)


Proton log - `vkCreateGraphicsPipeline` pop-up. this one seems much harder to trigger:
_will drop one here if i can get it to happen again_

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db5fe30f-b361-4f2b-a15e-f8b4af403098)

On a side note - on otherwise successful launches, the game's proton log can easily balloon to many gigabytes over time. Not sure if there's anything to be done there. Including a log captured from the middle of one of these successful launches:
[steam-2537590-successful.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17819582/steam-2537590-successful.tar.gz)

## Symptoms
After launching the game, it has a chance of randomly closing before completing the logos that play during the loading screen. This only happens on some launches and I'm not quite sure why. There is also a rarer issue where it launches an error window with a generic `vkCreateGraphicsPipeline` error.

The intro video only plays once per launch before showing a black screen during loading, ~~but this could be a game issue. I'll try checking Windows later today.~~
Edit: the video issue does not occur on Windows 

## Reproduction

1. Launch Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 under Proton Experimental bleeding-edge
2. Repeatedly launch the game, and close it if you make it to the "6% - Loading language..." screen
3. For the black screen after the loading video - this should happen after approx. 3 minutes
symptom_summary:
用户在启动《Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024》时，游戏有时会在加载屏幕的标志播放之前随机关闭，且偶尔会出现`vkCreateGraphicsPipeline`错误窗口。此外，成功启动后，游戏在加载时会显示黑屏，而介绍视频仅在每次启动时播放一次。

**Tag Suggestions:** Crash, Error Window, Black Screen, Video Playback Issue

compatibility_report:
A Plague Tale: Requiem (1182900) fails to launch on AMD + Mesa with vkCreateComputePipelines assertion

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/9030.
@enygma315 posted on 2025-09-07T00:25:30:

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: A Plague Tale: Requiem
- Steam AppID of the game: 1182900

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6950 XT (radeonsi, navi21)
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.1.5 (Pop!_OS 22.04)
- Kernel version: 6.12.10-76061203-generic
- Link to full system information report attached as steam_system_info.txt [steam_system_info.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22192353/steam_system_info.txt)

- Proton version: Proton 9.0.4, Proton Experimental, Proton Hotfix, GE-Proton10-8

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1182900.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22192369/steam-1182900.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

The game fails immediately on launch with the following Wine/Proton assertion error:
winevulkan/loader_thunks.c
Expression: "status && vkCreateComputePipelines"

The crash happens before the game reaches the main menu.

When forcing WineD3D with 
PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%
…the game behaves differently (black screen / crash) but avoids the Vulkan assertion. This suggests the issue is in the DX12 → Vulkan path (VKD3D-Proton + Mesa).

Other Unreal Engine 5 games on this system run without issue.


## Reproduction
Install A Plague Tale: Requiem

Launch with Proton Experimental (or other tested Proton versions)

Observe immediate crash with the vkCreateComputePipelines assertion


Proton log: attached as steam-1182900.log

Steam system information: attached as steam_system_info.txt
symptom_summary:

Tags: Crash, Launch Failure, Black Screen, Vulkan Issues

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: BlazBlue: Continuum Shift Extended
- Steam AppID of the game: 294810

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6600
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 23.1.3
- Kernel version: 6.4.3-zen
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/unit73e/ec35c7ee9f0780361d015d89e4c4de45
- Proton version: 8.0-2

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-294810.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12041926/steam-294810.log)

## Symptoms
Videos in story mode don't play, only the intro.

## Reproduction
There are two ways:
- Start Calamity Trigger story mode. The video does not play.
- Go to the gallery section and try playing a video.

Here is what happens:
![20230713180436_1](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/11358308/03172d6c-3a1b-43a7-9d51-816fe6b6f8f6)

## Debug
What I found so far:
- The videos can be found in `data/Movie` of the installation directory.
- The sound codec is `wmav2` and the video codec is `wmv3` for all videos, so gstreamer needs the libav plugin.
- The intro video plays correctly, but gallery videos don't play at all, despite having the same codecs.
- All videos play with ProtonGE8-6, but GE the sound does not work.
- Proton includes gstreamer libraries so it's not related to the OS.
- There's some kind of registry change in `compatdata/294810/gstreamer-1.0`.
- BlazBlue: Calamity Trigger didn't seem to have the same problem.
- Found out not all story mode videos fail, only some.
symptom_summary:
用户在《BlazBlue: Continuum Shift Extended》的故事模式中，视频无法播放，仅有开场动画正常显示。画廊部分的视频也无法播放，尽管它们与开场动画使用相同的编码格式。

**Tag Suggestions:** Video Playback Issues, Missing Features, Audio Problems

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Supreme Snowboarding - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_Snowboarding
- Steam AppID of the game: N/A - not on Steam or in SteamDB

## System Information
- GPU/Video driver version/Kernel version: SteamDeck
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): N/A - not sure how to generate on the SD
- Proton version: I tried Experimental, 8.0-3 and the default on SteamDeck as of the time of writing (not sure how to tell)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

No issues related to Supreme Snowboarding exist as of now. SteamDeck is up to date and I use several versions of Proton to no avail.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

The game won't start.

## Reproduction

Download SSB from https://supreme-snowboarding.eu/. It is a limited edition which is shareware but that already doesn't work so I don't think the full version does either. The full version is harder to come by.

Copy the installer to the Steam Deck using an SD card. Run Steam on the SteamDeck in Desktop Mode and add the installer as a new game. Run it with Proton Experimental - the installer will run and install the game. Find the installed game files in `home/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/meaninglessnumber/pfx/c_drive/Games/Supreme` and add it as a non-Steam game to Steam in Desktop Mode again.

Switch to Gaming Mode, set compatibility to Proton Experimental and attempt to run the game. It will crash right on startup.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Also, please copy the contents of `Help` > `Steam Runtime Information` to

6. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->
